message:
fix(api): add spam checking to support ticket replies

The POST handler for creating new support tickets already performed spam
checking via checkContentForSpam(), but the PUT handler for adding replies
to existing tickets was missing this protection. This created an inconsistency
where users could bypass spam detection by submitting spam content through
the reply flow instead of ticket creation.

This change adds the same spam checking behavior to the PUT handler:
- Checks reply content before processing
- Returns 400 with helpful error message if flagged as spam
- Adds internal note if content requires manual review

This ensures consistent moderation across all support ticket interactions
and closes a potential abuse vector.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
